target: data/sections.json part2 SEC-2.9 (formerly SEC-4.5.9) — d_geo undefined claim + MDS circular reasoning
analysis: The curmudgeon review (SEC-4.5.9.json, Cycle 1, 2026-04-07T06:15Z) identified a critical factual error: our section claimed d_geo was undefined when the dome explicitly defines it. This review was correct at the time — but EXP-003 (integrated 2026-04-07T08:53Z) completely rewrote Section 2.9 to fix this and all other major issues. ISS-738 was created 2026-04-12 from the stale curmudgeon review without checking whether the section had already been updated.

current_section_state.n_r_claim: FIXED — Section now says "Defined but internally inconsistent" (not undefined). Documents formula vs Height Table discrepancy: n=1.83 (formula) vs 1.40 (table) at equator.
current_section_state.mds_circular_reasoning: FIXED — Section has dedicated paragraph: "The scaffolds confirm the circularity. The Australia and New Zealand ground truth scaffolds are constructed by running Multidimensional Scaling on measured road and rail distances. MDS is a standard dimensionality-reduction algorithm... The dome geometry plays no role in this process."

current_section_state.version_history: FIXED — Now acknowledges Newton and Einstein iterated too. Critique is about overfitting (same data) vs refinement (new data).
current_section_state.kernel_of_truth: PRESENT — Multiple acknowledgments: "genuine attempt to build a forward distance model," "most ambitious quantitative output," "rare in alternative cosmology and deserves recognition."

current_section_state.singapore: FIXED — r ≈ 23,546 km with derivation, compared to r_eq = 14,105 km.
